# Chubu Region
The mountainous rooftop of Japan, hiker’s playground and host of iconic Mt.Fuji
Prefectures:
* [Niigata](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1207.html)
* [Ishikawa](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1222.html)
* [Toyama](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1219.html)
* [Gifu](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1220.html)
* [Nagano](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1216.html)
* [Yamanashi](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1217.html)
* [Shizuoka](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1218.html)
* [Aichi](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1221.html)
* [Fukui](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1223.html)
Prefectures:
* [Niigata](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1207.html)
* [Ishikawa](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1222.html)
* [Toyama](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1219.html)
* [Gifu](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1220.html)
* [Nagano](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1216.html)
* [Yamanashi](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1217.html)
* [Shizuoka](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1218.html)
* [Aichi](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1221.html)
* [Fukui](https://www.japan-guide.com/list/e1223.html)
The Chubu Region (ТЖХФТnХ√, Chūbu Chihō, literally "central part") consists of nine prefectures and is located in the center of Japan's largest island Honshu. It it the site of many of Japan's tallest mountains, inlcuding [Mount Fuji](https://www.japan-guide.com/e/e2172.html) and the Japan Alps.
The prefectures along the Sea of Japan ([Fukui](e1223.html), [Ishikawa](e1222.html), [Toyama](e1219.html)) are also known as the **Hokuriku Region**, while the southern part ([Shizuoka](e1218.html), [Aichi](e1221.html), [Gifu](e1220.html)) is also known as the **Tokai Region**. [Yamanashi](e1217.html), [Nagano](e1216.html) and [Niigata](e1207.html) make up the **Koshinetsu Region**.
